description: Enhance the admin document detail page with a sticky sidebar for artifact navigation, document health/completeness score, workflow API integration for artifact generation, and visual/UX polish

userStories.title: Add calculateDocumentHealth utility and artifactSectionId helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pure utility functions for health score calculation and consistent section ID generation so they can be used by both the sidebar and editor components.
acceptanceCriteria: calculateDocumentHealth function added to src/lib/document-utils.ts
Signature: calculateDocumentHealth(document: Document & { documentArtifact: DocumentArtifact[] }): HealthScoreResult

HealthScoreResult type exported with: score (0-100), maxScore (100), and breakdown object with requiredFields, optionalFields, artifacts, published — each having filled/missing string arrays and score/max numbers

Scoring: required fields 35 pts (7 each for title, originalDocumentUrl, dateSigned, signer, type), optional fields 10 pts (5 each for shortSummary, riskScore), artifact coverage 45 pts (5 per standard artifact from artifactOrder), published 10 pts
artifactSectionId(title: string) helper added: returns artifact-${title.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, '-')}
Both functions are pure with no side effects
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: src/lib/document-utils.ts. These are data-only utilities, no UI changes.

userStories.title: Add section IDs and canonical sort to document editor
userStories.description: As a developer, I need scroll-target IDs on each section in the document editor and artifacts sorted by canonical order so the sidebar can navigate to them.
acceptanceCriteria: id='document-details' added to the Document Details section element in document-editor.tsx
Each artifact div gets id={artifactSectionId(artifact.title)} attribute using the helper from US-001
Each artifact div gets scroll-mt-4 class for scroll offset
Artifacts sorted by artifactOrder index from document-utils.ts instead of alphabetical (.localeCompare)
Artifacts not in artifactOrder appear at the end of the list
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: src/app/admin/_components/document-editor.tsx. Import artifactSectionId and artifactOrder from ~/lib/document-utils.

userStories.title: Apply artifact border colors and icons in editor
userStories.description: As an admin, I want artifact cards in the editor to use their signature border colors and icons so I can visually distinguish artifact types at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Each artifact card uses border-l-4 with border color from getArtifactStyle(artifact.title).borderClass
Replaces current uniform border-gray-700 styling on artifact cards
Artifact icon from getArtifactStyle() displayed next to artifact title (h-4 w-4)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add published status badge to page header
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see a Draft/Published badge next to the document title so I know the visibility status at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Badge appears next to the document title h1 in src/app/admin/documents/[id]/page.tsx
Published documents show green badge with 'Published' text using Badge component with default variant
Unpublished documents show muted/gray badge with 'Draft' text using Badge component with secondary variant
Uses existing Badge component from ~/components/ui/badge
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_URL environment variable
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the workflow API base URL configurable via environment variable for deployment flexibility.
acceptanceCriteria: NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_URL added to src/env.js client schema with z.string().url().default('http://localhost:3200')
Added to runtimeEnv mapping: NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_URL: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_URL
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: src/env.js. Uses @t3-oss/env-nextjs createEnv pattern already in place.

userStories.title: Create DocumentSidebar component with health score card
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see a completeness score for the current document in a collapsible sidebar card so I know what's missing.
acceptanceCriteria: New client component created at src/app/admin/_components/document-sidebar.tsx
Health score card at top of sidebar using Card and Collapsible components
SVG circular progress ring with percentage (0-100) displayed in center
Color coded: green (80-100), yellow (60-79), orange (40-59), red (0-39)
Collapsed view shows ring + percentage only
Expanded view shows breakdown by category with mini progress bars and lists missing items
Uses calculateDocumentHealth from ~/lib/document-utils
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: src/app/admin/_components/document-sidebar.tsx. Props: document: Document & { documentArtifact: DocumentArtifact[] }. SVG ring uses stroke-dasharray/stroke-dashoffset technique.

userStories.title: Add artifact navigation list to sidebar
userStories.description: As an admin, I want all 9 standard artifact types listed in the sidebar with present/missing indicators so I can quickly assess coverage and jump to any section.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar lists 'Document Details' entry at top, followed by all 9 artifacts from artifactOrder
Each artifact shows its icon and border color from getArtifactStyle()
Present artifacts show a filled dot indicator; missing artifacts show a muted/hollow indicator
Clicking a present artifact smooth-scrolls to that section using scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' })
Clicking 'Document Details' scrolls to #document-details
Uses artifactSectionId() helper for consistent ID targeting
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: src/app/admin/_components/document-sidebar.tsx. Add to existing component from US-006.

userStories.title: Add active section highlighting to sidebar
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the sidebar to highlight which section I'm currently viewing so I maintain orientation while scrolling.
acceptanceCriteria: Active sidebar nav item has a left border accent and subtle background highlight
Active item determined by IntersectionObserver with rootMargin '-20% 0px -70% 0px'
Highlight updates smoothly as user scrolls through sections
Only one item highlighted at a time
Smooth 150ms transition on highlight changes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Restructure page layout to sidebar + main content
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the document detail page to have a sidebar + main content layout so navigation tools are always visible while I scroll.
acceptanceCriteria: Page uses flex layout: sidebar (280px, left) + main content (flex-1) with gap-6
Sidebar wrapper uses sticky top-4 self-start max-h-[calc(100vh-2rem)] overflow-y-auto
Main content area uses min-w-0 flex-1 and retains all existing functionality
Sidebar hidden below lg breakpoint via hidden lg:block; single-column layout preserved on mobile/tablet
Dark mode renders correctly
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: src/app/admin/documents/[id]/page.tsx. Import DocumentSidebar component. Wrap AdjacentDocs + DocumentEditor in a flex container with sidebar.

userStories.title: Add artifact generation buttons to sidebar
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to generate missing artifacts directly from the sidebar via the workflow API so I can quickly fill in content.
acceptanceCriteria: Missing artifacts in sidebar show a small 'Generate' icon button
Clicking Generate calls GET ${NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_URL}/document/<route>?documentId=<id> using env var from src/env.js

Route mapping: ELI5→/document/eli5, Key Points→/document/key-points, Constitutional Considerations→/document/constitutional-considerations, Take Action→/document/action-plan, Social Post→/document/social-post, Letter of Concern→/document/resist-letter, Risk Score Details→/document/score, Final Summary→/document/final-summary
Areas of Concern shows disabled generate button with tooltip 'Deprecated' (no API route)
While generating, button shows a spinner/loading state
On success, page refreshes via router.refresh() and artifact appears in nav and editor
On error, toast notification shows the error message using sonner
Generate All Missing button at bottom of artifact nav list calls GET /document/run-all?documentId=<id>
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: src/app/admin/_components/document-sidebar.tsx. Add artifact route mapping as a const record. Use fetch() for API calls. Import env from ~/env.

userStories.title: Add keyboard shortcut for edit mode toggle
userStories.description: As a power user, I want to press Ctrl+E to toggle edit mode on the document details section without reaching for the mouse.
acceptanceCriteria: Ctrl+E (or Cmd+E on Mac) toggles the document details edit mode
Only active when not already in a text input, textarea, or editing an artifact
Uses useEffect with keydown listener checking e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey and e.key === 'e'
Calls e.preventDefault() to prevent browser default behavior
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: src/app/admin/_components/document-editor.tsx. Check document.activeElement.tagName to skip when in input/textarea.
